subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (from rulebook)
- **2 Players (multiplayer)** — Draft Pool each round is 5 dice.
- **3 Players (multiplayer)** — Draft Pool each round is 7 dice.
- **4 Players (multiplayer)** — Draft Pool each round is 9 dice.
- **2/3/4 Players setup note** — Core setup is the same for 2, 3, or 4 players.
- **Solo mode** — Uses 4 dice per round, no Favor Tokens, 2 Public Objectives face up, 2 Private Objectives face up, and 1-5 Tool Cards based on difficulty.

#### Player Windows & Boards
- **Window Pattern Cards** — 12 cards.
- **Window Frame Player Boards** — 4 boards.

#### Dice & Bag
- **Dice total** — 90 dice.
- **Color split** — 18 red, 18 yellow, 18 green, 18 blue, 18 purple.
- **Dice Bag** — 1 bag.

#### Objective & Tool Cards
- **Tool Cards** — 12 cards.
- **Public Objective Cards** — 10 cards.
- **Private Objective Cards** — 5 cards.

#### Tokens & Tracks
- **Favor Tokens** — 24 tokens.
- **Score Markers** — 4 markers.
- **Round Track / Score Track** — 1 dual-sided track (Round front, Score back).

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Shared Table Setup
1. **Round track** — Place the Round Track near the center of play.
2. **Tool display** — Shuffle Tool Cards and reveal 3 face up.
3. **Public display** — Shuffle Public Objective Cards and reveal 3 face up.
4. **Dice bag** — Put all 90 dice into the Dice Bag.
5. **Unused cards** — Set aside all unused objective, tool, and window cards.

#### Play Space Tips
- **Draft Pool area** — Leave clear space near center for rolled dice each round.
- **Round leftovers** — Leave room on Round Track spaces for unpicked dice.
- **Scoring flip** — Keep space to flip the Round Track to its Score side at endgame.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Per-Player Setup
1. **Private objective** — Deal 1 Private Objective card face down to each player.
2. **Window options** — Deal 2 random Window Pattern cards to each player.
3. **Frame board** — Give each player 1 Window Frame Player Board.
4. **Pattern choice** — Choose 1 of the 4 possible patterns (front/back of both cards).
5. **Pattern insert** — Slide chosen pattern into the bottom of the frame board.
6. **Favor tokens** — Take Favor Tokens equal to chosen pattern difficulty (3 to 6).
7. **Score marker** — Place your matching Score Marker next to the Round Track.

#### Difficulty Reminder
- **Easy windows** — Difficulty 3 gives fewer Favor Tokens.
- **Hard windows** — Difficulty 6 gives more Favor Tokens.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Start Player
- **Selection** — Randomly choose the Start Player.
- **Dice Bag** — Give the Dice Bag to the Start Player.
- **Rulebook suggestion** — Most recent cathedral visitor may be Start Player.

#### Draft Pool by Player Count
--- 2 Players ---
- **Dice rolled each round** — 5 dice.
- **Formula** — 2 per player + 1 extra.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Dice rolled each round** — 7 dice.
- **Formula** — 2 per player + 1 extra.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Dice rolled each round** — 9 dice.
- **Formula** — 2 per player + 1 extra.

#### Setup Consistency
- **Same setup structure** — The setup procedure is otherwise the same for 2, 3, and 4 players.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Game Length
- **Fixed length** — The game lasts 10 rounds.

#### Round Sequence
1. **Roll draft dice** — Start Player draws and rolls dice to create the Draft Pool.
2. **Clockwise pass** — Each player takes one turn in clockwise order.
3. **Counterclockwise pass** — Starting with last player, each player takes a second turn in reverse order.
4. **Place leftovers** — Put all remaining Draft Pool dice on the current round space of the Round Track.
5. **Pass bag** — Pass the Dice Bag clockwise; that player is next round’s Start Player.
6. **Repeat** — Continue until round 10 is complete.
7. **Endgame** — After round 10, proceed to scoring.

#### Draft Pool by Player Count
--- 2 Players ---
- **Draft Pool size** — 5 dice each round.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Draft Pool size** — 7 dice each round.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Draft Pool size** — 9 dice each round.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Actions (Any Order)
- **Action A: Draft & place** — Take 1 die from Draft Pool and place it on your window.
- **Action B: Use tool** — Spend Favor Tokens to use 1 Tool Card.
- **Optionality** — You may do both actions, one action, or pass.

#### Die Placement Rules
- **First die placement** — Your first die of the game must be on an edge or corner space.
- **Adjacency after first die** — Every later die must touch a placed die orthogonally or diagonally.
- **Color restriction** — Colored spaces require that exact die color.
- **Value restriction** — Numbered spaces require that exact die value.
- **White spaces** — White spaces accept any color/value if all other rules are obeyed.
- **Orthogonal conflict (color)** — No two orthogonally adjacent dice may share color.
- **Orthogonal conflict (value)** — No two orthogonally adjacent dice may share value.

#### Tool Card Costs
- **First use of that tool** — Pay 1 Favor Token if no tokens are on that Tool Card.
- **Later use of that tool** — Pay 2 Favor Tokens if that Tool Card already has tokens.
- **Token placement** — Place paid Favor Tokens on the Tool Card.
- **Use limit per turn** — You may use only 1 Tool Card on your turn.

#### Passing
- **Draft pass** — You may skip drafting a die.
- **Tool pass** — You may skip tool use.
- **Full pass** — You may do neither action.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Snake Draft Tempo
- **Forward pass** — First half of round proceeds clockwise.
- **Reverse pass** — Second half proceeds counterclockwise.
- **Timing impact** — Turn position has both early and late value in the same round.

#### Objective Layers
- **Shared race** — All players score the same 3 Public Objectives.
- **Private scoring** — Each player also scores a secret color-based Private Objective.

#### Tool Economy
- **Escalating access** — A Tool Card is cheaper for first user (1 token) than later users (2 tokens).
- **Value timing** — Tool effects tend to become more powerful as windows fill.

#### Placement Correction Rule
- **Illegal window check** — If a window breaks placement rules, correct immediately.
- **Correction method** — Remove dice of your choice until window is legal.
- **Removed dice status** — Removed dice are out of play.
- **Score impact** — Any resulting empty spaces still count as penalties.

#### Solo Mode (Official)
- **Favor Tokens** — Not used.
- **Objective display** — 2 Public Objectives face up and 2 Private Objectives face up.
- **Tool count by difficulty** — Use 1 Tool Card (most challenging) up to 5 Tool Cards (easiest).
- **Rounds** — Still 10 rounds.
- **Dice per round** — Roll 4 dice.
- **Turns per round** — Take 2 turns.
- **Tool payment** — Spend a Draft Pool die matching the Tool Card color.
- **Solo tool limit** — Each Tool Card may be used only once in solo.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Game end** — The game ends after the 10th round.

#### Scoring Steps
1. **Flip track** — Clear dice from Round Track and flip to Score Track side.
2. **Track totals** — Use Score Markers; flip markers if a score exceeds 50.
3. **Add points** — Score all VP sources.
4. **Determine winner** — Highest total VP wins.

#### VP Sources (Multiplayer)
- **Public Objectives** — Score each of the 3 Public Objective Cards.
- **Public repeat scoring** — You may score each public card multiple times when applicable.
- **Row/column condition** — Row/column objectives score only completed rows/columns (no open spaces).
- **Private Objective** — Sum pip values of dice matching your private color.
- **Unspent Favor Tokens** — Score +1 VP per remaining token.
- **Open spaces penalty** — Lose 1 VP per empty space.

#### Tiebreakers (in order)
- **Tiebreaker 1** — Most points from Private Objective.
- **Tiebreaker 2** — Most remaining Favor Tokens.
- **Tiebreaker 3** — Reverse player order in the final round.

#### Solo Endgame
- **Target Score** — Sum values of all dice on Round Track before flipping it.
- **Excluded dice** — Dice spent to pay for Tool Cards are not counted in Target Score.
- **Solo open-space penalty** — Lose 3 VP per empty space.
- **Solo private choice** — Score using only one of the two face-up Private Objectives.
- **Solo victory** — Win if your VP total is greater than Target Score.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### First 2 Rounds Priorities
- **Start-space flexibility** — Place your first die on an edge/corner that keeps multiple legal follow-ups open.
- **Constraint mapping** — Identify 2-3 hardest spaces on your window (tight value/color demands) before your first draft.
- **Lane protection** — Keep at least one legal adjacency path toward each hard space.
- **Snake planning** — Draft with both your first and second turn of the round in mind.
- **Tool discipline** — Spend early Favor Tokens only when a tool fixes board structure, not just for +1 pip style gains.

#### Player-Count Draft Pressure
--- 2 Players ---
- **Pool size** — 5 dice means options collapse quickly; prioritize legal flexibility over perfect values.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Pool size** — 7 dice allows tactical positioning; prepare a primary pick and one backup before your turn.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Pool size** — 9 dice gives broad options early, but competition for key colors/values is highest; reserve flexibility for reverse-order pick.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Orthogonal confusion** — Applying same-color/value bans to diagonal neighbors is incorrect and reduces legal options.
- **Overfocusing one public card** — Committing too early can dead-end your layout when draft values shift.
- **Token overspending** — Burning Favor Tokens on low-impact tool uses costs both tempo and endgame VP.
- **Late-space trap** — Leaving awkward isolated spaces until round 8-10 often creates unavoidable penalties.
- **Adjacency blind spots** — Placing a die that blocks future legal entries into required spaces is a common self-lock.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Biggest Trade-Offs
- **Public vs private** — Decide when to chase repeatable Public Objective sets versus high pip totals in your private color.
- **Tokens now vs points later** — Decide whether immediate tool intervention is worth losing guaranteed +1 VP per unspent token.
- **Safety vs ceiling** — Choose between a safe legal placement that preserves future options and a high-value placement that may constrain the rest of your grid.

metadata.notes: Primary source: provided Sagrada v1.2 rulebook text including setup, gameplay, tool/favor economy, endgame scoring, tie-breakers, placement correction rule, and solo-mode differences.
